why_not_result_improving: This adds non-overfitting validation pressure and pairwise repair replay outside the locked denominator; it does not change fixture labels, baselines, metrics, RQs, or BEP-Deep/BEP-Max outcomes.

affected_section: Source-derived boundary benchmark and representation-invariance stress
motivation: A strong benchmark review found that SpecBench and BEP-Shadow should exercise not only single perturbations but also composite representation changes that combine normalization, irrelevant metadata, cache/reporting fields, and context expansion.
change: Expanded BEP-SpecBench from 1392 to 2755 source-derived boundary/composite-stress cases and expanded BEP-Shadow from six to ten required representation-preserving transformations, increasing required shadow replays from 5832 to 9720 with zero failures.
data_observed_before_change: The earlier release release already passed 1392 SpecBench cases and 5832 required shadow replays; those were strong but still mostly single-perturbation workloads.
why_not_result_improving: The added cases remain outside the BEP-Deep empirical denominator and are generated from semantics-preserving transformations; they do not add empirical positives, remove failures, reinterpret baselines, or alter headline counts.

affected_section: Theory presentation, scale stress, and release validation synchronization
motivation: A formal/reproducibility review found that the finite theorem kernel should have compact proof cards that exactly account for the executable theorem obligations, and that scale replay should be deepened before the repository is considered best-paper-grade.
change: Added a proof-card audit for all 30 theorem obligations, checking claim/proof-kind/state accounting against the theorem kernel; increased BEP-Scale replay to 48600 deterministic stress cases; synchronized release validation, reproduction ladder, paper, supplement, result index, and checksum manifest with 52 validation layers.
data_observed_before_change: The earlier release release had 30 theorems over 33513 states, 24300 scale-stress cases, and 49 validation layers; proof accounting was available in JSON but not separately validated as evidence-facing proof cards.
why_not_result_improving: This strengthens proof presentation and replay workload without changing the locked denominator, labels, baseline scope, RQs, or semantic results.

motivation: A fresh methodology review found that preserving negative controls and clearing repairs did not by itself prove that clean controls sit on the intended semantic boundary; assessors could ask whether the oracle only recognizes positives already present in BEP-Deep.
change: Added causal counterfactual activation over activatable clean controls: paired repairs are reversed to their positives and clean controls in encoded intent classes receive targeted causal edits; 546/546 required activations cross the semantic boundary and emit the expected issue.
data_observed_before_change: The earlier release had 554 clean controls, 418 repair-delta pairs, 4860 identifier-blind replays, and 9720 shadow replays, but no complementary activation gate from clean controls to issue-emitting counterfactuals.
why_not_result_improving: The audit creates out-of-denominator counterfactuals and does not relabel locked controls, delete failures, change baselines, or move any RQ/metric/denominator.

motivation: A baseline review found that the paper should not only report full public-package execution but also quantify why scanner-style flags are contrastive evidence rather than intent-drift labels.
change: Added an external contrast-specificity audit over 4118 public-package comparator rows, computing fixture-level flag behavior for all 418 semantic positives and 554 clean controls and documenting that raw comparator flags are not used as BEP labels.
data_observed_before_change: The earlier release had full public-package comparator outputs with zero unavailable/error rows, but it did not separately quantify comparator specificity over the locked semantic denominator.
why_not_result_improving: This analysis interprets already materialized external outputs; it does not create new semantic positives, replace the BEP oracle, or tune results by comparator agreement.

affected_section: Benchmark-adjacent independence and deterministic runtime closure

motivation: A strict program-analysis review found that two executable oracles and metadata-erasure replays still left a possible attack: semantic decision code might accidentally consult benchmark labels or lack a fully declarative third oracle.

motivation: A benchmark review found that several locked issue classes had no direct source-derived SpecBench pressure, so aggregate SpecBench counts could hide per-class evidence gaps.

change: Updated release validation to 99 materialized layers, reproduction ladder to 89 declared commands, package identity to 0.22.0, protocol amendments A001-A087, and added a conservative release assessor scorecard across novelty, rigor, reproducibility, anti-overfit evidence, paper surface, and reference integrity.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had 90 validation layers, 80 reproduction commands, package 0.21.0, and no criteria/scorecard closure.
why_not_result_improving: release synchronization and self-review only; locked denominator, labels, baselines, metrics, RQs, and headline results remain unchanged.

motivation: After abstract registration, the paper and artifact need a stable capsule connecting the aligned title, abstract, primary area, additional area, and selected areas to the main paper surface.
change: Added paper_metadata.json and an executable alignment audit over title, abstract terms, topics, and anonymity boundary.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had paper-surface audits but no paper delivery-metadata capsule.
why_not_result_improving: delivery-surface consistency only; no labels, denominator, baselines, metrics, or RQs change.

motivation: A assessor can reject a strong artifact if the first reproducibility path is unclear or appears to require private services.
change: Added quickstart.md and a quickstart-readiness audit covering short, medium, and full deterministic review lanes.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had a long ladder but no concise assessor quickstart capsule.
why_not_result_improving: Navigation hardening only; computations and results are unchanged.

motivation: A presentation assessor could notice an unreferenced LaTeX-native figure or unresolved reference even if the PDF compiles.
change: Added a figure/table/algorithm reference-closure audit and fixed the method-pipeline figure reference in the main text.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had figure layout auditing but not reference closure; the method-pipeline figure was not explicitly referenced.
why_not_result_improving: Presentation and traceability hardening only; no empirical result changes.

affected_section: Main supplement and artifact count synchronization
motivation: Main paper, supplement, README, and reproduction surfaces can drift after late validation changes.
change: Added supplement-alignment auditing over current 110-layer, 100-command, reference, SpecBench, oracle, and evidence-card counts.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had stale-number audits but not a four-surface alignment gate for the release registration metadata.
why_not_result_improving: Release consistency only; no labels or metrics move.

motivation: A reproducibility assessor could find that a capsule entry or result-index row points to a missing file.
change: Added artifact-manifest reachability auditing over required capsule files, result index rows, and checksum manifest size.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release checked capsule presence but not the new quickstart/metadata surfaces and result-index reachability together.
why_not_result_improving: Packaging hardening only; no scientific result changes.

motivation: Late edits to evidence cards, claim traces, repairs, graph metrics, or references should be tamper-evident.
change: Added a canonical digest audit over the core source-claim, evidence-card, repair, graph, and reference surfaces.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had hash-chain auditing for witnesses but not a compact digest over the release evidence pack.
why_not_result_improving: Integrity hardening only; no labels or conclusions change.

motivation: A large artifact can be correct but still hard to review under time pressure.
change: Added a assessor triage index and audit mapping common objections to concrete paper/artifact surfaces.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had entrypoint auditing but not an objection-to-evidence map.
why_not_result_improving: Navigation hardening only; no experiment changes.

motivation: The submitted work is judged through main paper, supplement, and anonymous artifact link; these must close together.
change: Added release delivery-packet auditing over main PDF, supplement PDF, metadata/quickstart/triage surfaces, and 110-layer release validation.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had deliverable-trio readiness but not the new registration/quickstart/digest surfaces.
why_not_result_improving: release delivery hardening only; no denominator, labels, metrics, baselines, or RQs move.

motivation: A release research surface can accidentally sound stronger than the evidence supports, especially after repeated polishing.
change: Added overclaim-boundary auditing over main paper, supplement, README, reproduction protocol, and study protocol; the audit rejects unqualified prevalence, exploitability, human-study, private-data, GPU, and full-browser-conformance claims.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had anonymity and rhetoric audits but not a semantic overclaim boundary gate over the release surfaces.
why_not_result_improving: Scope guard only; no denominator, labels, metrics, baselines, or RQs move.

motivation: LaTeX source audits can miss text introduced through included tables or compiled PDFs.
change: Added a compiled-PDF text audit that extracts main and supplement PDF text and rejects process traces, unresolved references, local paths, and assessor-process language.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release caught source-level rhetoric issues but did not inspect compiled PDF text directly.
why_not_result_improving: Presentation and hygiene hardening only; no scientific result changes.

motivation: A reproducibility assessor may question whether public-source grounding or external-resource rights are explicit enough.
change: Added public-provenance auditing over source ledgers, source snapshots, external resources, environment lock, and package boundary flags.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had source-span closure and reference integrity but not a release data-rights/public-provenance gate.
why_not_result_improving: Provenance hardening only; no result or baseline-scope changes.

motivation: The release anonymous repository will be uploaded manually, so the artifact tree needs a stable include/exclude manifest and pre-upload checks.
change: Added repository_upload_manifest.json and an audit checking BEPGuard upload root, required files, cache exclusion, and pre-upload validation commands.
data_observed_before_change: The prior release had delivery-packet readiness but not a manifest for manual repository upload.
why_not_result_improving: Packaging and upload-readiness hardening only; no denominator, labels, metrics, baselines, or RQs move.
